name: whatsapp-mcp-docker
description: WhatsApp MCP server for Docker MCP Toolkit — send messages, search chats, approval workflows, and remote agent control via any MCP client

scripts._guard: node -e "if(process.env.NODE_ENV!=='test'){console.error('\u26a0\ufe0f  Run tests inside the container: docker compose run --rm tester-container npm run test:all');process.exit(1)}"
scripts.test: npm run _guard && find test/unit -name '*.test.ts' -o -name '*.test.js' | xargs tsx --test && find test/integration -name '*.test.ts' -o -name '*.test.js' | xargs tsx --test
scripts.test:unit: npm run _guard && find test/unit -name '*.test.ts' -o -name '*.test.js' | xargs tsx --test
scripts.test:integration: npm run _guard && find test/integration -name '*.test.ts' -o -name '*.test.js' | xargs tsx --test
scripts.test:all: npm run _guard && find test/unit test/integration -name '*.test.ts' -o -name '*.test.js' | xargs tsx --test
scripts.test:coverage: npm run _guard && c8 --reporter=text --reporter=lcov --all --src=src -- tsx --test $(find test/unit test/integration -name '*.test.ts' -o -name '*.test.js')
scripts.docker:test: docker compose --profile test build tester-container && docker compose --profile test run --rm tester-container
scripts.docker:test:auth: docker compose run --rm tester-container npm run test:auth
scripts.docker:test:e2e: docker compose run --rm tester-container npm run test:e2e
